response.justification: The candidate is a capable full-stack and systems engineer with 15+ years of broad experience, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role. Professional C++ usage amounts to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, no SQL Server or Oracle work, no fixed income or capital markets domain knowledge, and no mention of Summit, STL, or financial messaging frameworks. The candidate's primary expertise sits firmly in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile systems, none of which satisfy the mandatory requirements.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience with strong testing culture (TDD, trunk-based development) and solid CI/CD and DevOps background
Demonstrated communication and knowledge-sharing through conference talks, workshops, and open-source project leadership
Some Java exposure and scripting/tooling experience provide marginal overlap with optional desirable skills
concerns: C++ experience is negligible for this role — approximately six months professional use at NI (2014–2015) and a thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
Zero fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge; RIDE Capital exposure was purely TypeScript/GraphQL product work unrelated to financial instruments
No SQL Server or Oracle experience, no Windows platform development, and no mention of Summit, Kafka, or MQ
